description: CodeBoarding is an agentic static analysis framework that converts complex source code repositories into high-level architectural documentation and interactive diagrams. The main flow coordinates specialized LLM agents (Abstraction, Details, Meta) via an Agentic Orchestration layer to interpret and present code intent, while a Static Analysis Engine produces structural ground-truth (call graphs, symbol tables) using language-specific adapters. A Knowledge Base & Persistence layer stores and increments analysis state and resolves repo references, Execution Monitoring tracks performance and LLM usage, and Testing & Diagnostics validate the end-to-end pipeline. The purpose is to produce accurate, up-to-date architectural documentation and visualizations informed by both static analysis and LLM reasoning.

components.name: Agentic Orchestration & Presentation
components.description: The central reasoning hub that manages the lifecycle of analysis jobs, coordinating specialized LLM agents to interpret code structure and transforming raw analysis data into visual formats like Mermaid diagrams, Markdown, and HTML.

components.name: Static Analysis Engine
components.description: The core structural analysis component that interfaces with LSP clients to build comprehensive call graphs and symbol tables, using language-specific adapters to handle Python, Java, TypeScript, Go, and PHP.

components.name: Knowledge Base & Persistence
components.description: Manages long-term storage of analysis results (DuckDB and local caches), handles incremental change detection to avoid redundant processing, and provides utilities for resolving code references and normalizing repository paths.

components.name: Execution Monitoring
components.description: An observability layer that tracks LLM token usage, tool execution times, and provides tracing across the agentic workflow using context managers and callbacks for real-time statistics.

components.name: Testing & Diagnostics
components.description: A suite of unit and integration tests and diagnostic tools (including mock projects with edge cases) for validating call graph integrity, LSP communication, and end-to-end agent workflows.

components_relations.relation: requests structural data and call graphs
retrieves cached analysis results and stores generated documentation/diagrams
uses reference resolution utilities to map symbols to file paths and persists structural metadata
reports agent actions and LLM token consumption for usage tracking
logs performance metrics and LSP interaction timings
validates end-to-end agent workflows and documentation generation
exercises the engine against edge-case projects to ensure call graph accuracy
